what_it_does: Introduces the company project as an interface-driven composition suite engine, states its target behavior, declares the repo is AI-built, maps directory responsibilities to contracts/specs, sketches the eventual Python/TypeScript runtime architecture, and tells readers to treat an external build-prep vault as the source of truth.
observations: The build target is an interface-driven composition suite for composing, configuring, running, and steering AI chains over sources.
The repo is described as built and grown entirely by AI, with no human developers.
AI agents are directed to read AGENTS.md, then MAP.md, then module-level AGENTS.md.
The source of truth is an external build-prep vault, not this README.
Directory layout is mapped to contracts C1–C8 and specs S1–S7.
Planned backend is Python (runtime/ with Yjs sync server + action endpoint); frontend is TS/Tauri in canvas/.
Storage is intended to live on the WSL ext4 side for real fsync.
First milestone is a walking skeleton: one node → runtime → result, with real store and bridge, no AI.
Status is explicitly skeleton only; build proceeds stage-gated per decision D7.
The directory is named mcp_face instead of mcp to avoid colliding with the mcp SDK.
standards: Explicit AI-only maintenance model with module constitutions and contract-driven boundaries.
External build-prep vault is declared the canonical source of truth.
The project is currently only a skeleton; nothing is implemented.
mcp_face naming shows awareness of SDK namespace collision.
conventions: Uses Markdown with header hierarchy and blockquote callouts.
Uses a table to map directories to responsibilities and governing contracts/specs.
Uses emoji status indicator and stage-gated milestone language.
References external documents by filename rather than embedding their contents.
concerns: The README defers nearly all detail to an external vault, so it risks becoming stale or contextless if the vault path changes.
It states nothing is implemented, so it cannot be used to understand working code.
Heavy reliance on contract/spec identifiers (C1–C8, S1–S7, D1–D7) without defining them locally may confuse readers who lack the vault.
notes: This file is the landing orientation for an AI-built composition engine, pointing readers to external specs and declaring the repo is currently an unimplemented skeleton.
questions: What is the exact filesystem path or URI to the build-prep vault from this repo?
What does Vi composition suite mean in this context?
What are the contents of contracts C1–C8 and specs S1–S7?
Who or what is Tim, and why is independence from Tim important?
What is the precise scope and acceptance criteria of the walking skeleton milestone?
Is mcp_face a Python package, a FastMCP server, or both?
What do process · content · presentation types mean for nodes/?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated purpose as a project orientation README, though it functions more as an index to external documents than as a self-contained description of the codebase.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a navigable, AI-maintainable codebase where every module has a constitution and composes against pinned contracts, while postponing implementation until stage-gated milestones are reached.
novelty: Explicitly frames the repo as maintained entirely by AI agents with module constitutions; treats an external build-prep vault as the canonical source of truth; uses mcp_face as a directory name to avoid an SDK namespace collision.
contribution: Provides the project's identity, high-level goals, directory-to-contract boundary map, agent navigation instructions, and explicit skeleton status, which a fused system would keep as the entry orientation.
summary_for_embedding: README.md for the company project, an AI-built interface-driven composition suite engine, outlining goals, module boundaries tied to contracts and specs, planned Python and TypeScript architecture, and skeleton status while directing readers to an external build-prep vault.
intention_for_embedding: Orient AI agents and future contributors to the project's purpose, layout, and external source of truth before implementation begins.
